wiki.title: Copy and Collaborate Policy
wiki.body_html:
At AnnieCannons, we are all learning together. We want you to work together on your coding problems, but we do have a few boundaries and expectations where this is concerned.

For students in ITP and advanced modules:

  • You can NOT  fork or turn in another person's work as your own.
    • Do not give others permission to copy your code.
  • You are encouraged to work together. However, when you do:
    • Make a comment in the code containing a list of student names who worked together.
    • All students involved should have the same comment in their code when they turn it in.

 

For students in advanced modules:

  • Same as above, and…
  • When copying code from the internet:
    • Cite your source: provide a URL for the code. 
      • It also gives the developer credit for their work!
      • You will be able to look at that site later if you don't remember what that code does.
    • Include a comment explaining what the code does.
  • If you are concerned whether something you're doing is violating our rules, ask your instructor for clarification.

 

Approach to Addressing Policy Concerns

If a suspected violation of the policy arises, here’s what you can expect:

 

  • We’ll start by having a conversation to better understand the situation, giving you the opportunity to explain your approach or offer any context.
  • If it’s determined that the work submitted does not align with our guidelines, we will ask you to remove the assignment and redo it. 
    • This gives you the opportunity to demonstrate your understanding through a fresh effort.
  • As part of this process, your instructor may ask you to walk through your revised code, line by line, to ensure comprehension and help reinforce your learning.

Be sure to check out AnnieCannons' spreadsheet of online job platforms! It also includes tabs for professional networking platforms and additional resources. Feel free to make a copy if you'd like to edit or customize it. AnnieCannons JOB PLATFORMS 

Reminder: Tech communities and forums are excellent ways to engage with like-minded professionals. They offer opportunities to learn, seek guidance on technical topics, and stay updated on industry trends and best practices.

wiki.title: Technical Interview Prep Resources & Planning
wiki.body_html:
Technical Interview Prep ResourcesLinks to an external site.

Remember that these resources and tools can support your job search and career development in the tech sector. It's important to explore and utilize those that align with your specific goals and interests. In preparation as you apply for roles, remember to ask the recruiter/person of contact what type of prep should be done and what the technical questions will focus on.
Always stay curious, keep learning, and leverage these resources to enhance your technical competence and interview performance. Good luck!

REMEMBER THAT NOTHING NEEDS TO BE PAID FOR—ALL RESOURCES ARE FREE!

wiki.title: What to do in Week 7
wiki.body_html:
Monday

On Monday, you'll finish your Cannons Coffee project.

  • Start with your to-do list from Thursday last week. Work on the items you've outlined that need to be completed.
  • In Block D, we'll stop to talk about the project, reflect on learning, and do the Week 6 blogging pertaining to Cannons Coffee.

Tuesday

  • Today you will review all of your past assignments.
    • Complete any assignments you have not finished.
    • Redo any assignments with a grade of "incomplete" and re-submit them for re-grading.
    • If you have not finished Cannons Coffee, finish it!
  • Lotta, an AC grad, will speak in Block C.

Wednesday 

  • You'll be watching a Frontend Masters course focused on getting prepared for job hunting, like getting your LinkedIn and Github profiles together, preparing for interviews, etc.
  • You'll complete an assignment attached to the videos.

Thursday

  • You'll work with Dawn in the first three blocks to create materials suggested in the Frontend Masters couse.
    • You'll set up an Indeed profile.
    • You'll create a first draft of a resume using Indeed's templates
    • You'll work on your LinkedIn profile
  • You'll complete blogging for this week.

By the end of this week

You should be completely caught up with all HTML and CSS work, ready for JavaScript which starts next week!

wiki.title: AI Prompt library & resources
wiki.body_html:
✨ How to Use This  AI Resource

Explore the shared prompts, tools, resources, and workflows others have documented to see what’s possible with AI. AI Prompt library & resources : TEMPLATE  (*make your own copy if you like)

This is a living collection of ideas to help you discover how AI can support your work. Browse examples to spark your own experiments, adapt what others have tried, and contribute back when you find something that works for you.

Remember:

  • Not every tool or workflow is right for every context—use your judgment and keep our data privacy and security policies in mind.

  • As coders and learners, AI can be a powerful aid—but it doesn’t replace foundational problem-solving or critical thinking. Use it thoughtfully to complement your skills, not shortcut them.

wiki.title: Bento Grid Instructions
wiki.body_html:
Create the following design. You may start in CodePen, then switch to VS Code, then push your design to Github and Github Pages. Alternatively, you may start working in VS Code directly.

Mobile design: 

 

Desktop design:

All project images to download plus the mobile and desktop images shown: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1f349iqXeNRSnyWEuV6F33LZeMT8cdsZv/view?usp=sharing 

 

Suggested approach

  • Build your mobile layout first.
    • Start in CodePen if you want: https://codepen.io/team/anniecannons/pen/bGXryog 
    • Or start in VS Code directly. Download the images for the project: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1f349iqXeNRSnyWEuV6F33LZeMT8cdsZv/view?usp=sharing 
    • Set up a series of cards. Mark up the cards with the appropriate HTML and images.
    • Style each card using the appropriate colors, fonts, images. (See style guide below for details.)
    • Get your design as close as possible to the design shown above.
  • Now build the desktop layout.
    • Apply CSS Grid to make this layout. Remember to figure out how many rows and columns are needed.
    • Adjust your font size if needed.

Style guide information

The designs were created to the following widths:
Mobile: 375px 
Desktop: 1440px

What breakpoints should you use? Take a a look at your card designs. Where does it look best? Set your breakpoint there to transition from mobile to desktop layout. If you want to include an intermediate tablet layout, that's a great thing to do.


Colors
Light Purple: #dacffc
Dark Purple: #7650dc
Light Yellow: #f9eee1
Gold: #ffcb6b 
White: #ffffff
Black: #121212

Fonts
Font size (paragraph): 1.125rem
Font Family: DM Sans
Weights: 400, 500

wiki.title: Typemaster instructions
wiki.body_html:
To complete the Typemaster challenge, follow these instructions.

0. Get the files 

Optional CodePen starting point: https://codepen.io/team/anniecannons/pen/KKOZVOK 

Download the website images plus the images that show how the mobile, tablet, and desktop designs should look, plus the optional Figma file, from here: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1DhkGtNJYu11lkjxVts4PZNiILJVugttf?usp=sharing 

1. Build the design system

The design system describes your colors and fonts. You can set this up in your CSS using variables.

2. Build the mobile layout

Stack all of the information on top of each other. Do the simple things first (probably the icons plus text at the bottom) and the harder things last (probably the image layout on top).

3. Build tablet and desktop layout

Remember to start mobile first, then tablet, then desktop!

4. Post on Github and Github Pages

 

I want an extra challenge

There's a Figma file that will describe exactly how the design should look. Download the Figma application, then use the Figma file to dictate how the site would look.

wiki.title: Thursday: Preparing for Cannons Coffee Inquiry Page and Login Page
wiki.body_html:
Today, we'll be building an inquiry page and a login page for Cannons Coffee. To get started, let's get our coding environment configured and ready for work.

  1. Open VS Code and open your Cannons Coffee project in VS Code.
  2. Create a new file and name it inquiry.html. Copy all the HTML code from latte.html and paste it into inquiry.html
  3. In the body of inquiry.html, delete all content inside the main tags
  4. Create a new file and name it login.html. Copy all the HTML code from inquiry.html and paste it into login.html
  5. Go to all your html files, and add links to inquiry.html and login.html in the navigation
  6. Create a folder called js. This folder should be at the same level as your css and images folder.
  7. Inside of the js folder, create 2 files called custom-inquiry.js and custom-login.js
  8. Link custom-inquiry.js to inquiry.html and custom-login.js to login.html. You may find this resource useful: How to Add JavaScript to HTML for Beginners | DigitalOcean (look for method 3)

 

There is nothing to turn in. This will get you prepared to build the contact page that's today's project.
wiki.manifest_title: Thursday: Preparing for Cannons Coffee Inquiry Page and Login Page

wiki.title: HTML reference (from TCF)
wiki.body_html:
HTML element/tags What it does Example 
<h1></h1>
<h2></h2>
<h3></h3>
<h4></h4>
<h5></h5>
<h6></h6>

Headings! These identify sections of your web page. 

There can only be one <h1> on your web page. This is the most important thing on the page.

You can have as many of the other headings as you want.

 
<h1>About Apples</h1>

<h2>Types of Apples</h2>

<h3>New England Apples</h3>

<h2>Apple Pie</h2>
<p></p> Paragraphs! Your basic blah blah blah. <p>Make the pie crust.</p>
<ul></ul> Unordered list - this is a list of items that can go in any order. An unordered list MUST contain list items (at least one!). 
<ul>
   <li>Honey Crisp</li>
   <li>Red Delicious</li>
</ul>
<ol></ol> Ordered list - these items must go in a certain order. Use this for instructions or steps. 
<ol>
   <li>Step 1</li>
   <li>Step 2</li>
</ol>
<li></li> List item - these are the items inside of your ordered or unordered list See examples above
<!-- --> Comment -- leave yourself notes in the code. This will not display on the website. 
<!-- secret message here! -->

<a></a>

 

Links! ("anchor", thus the A in the tag.) Use this if you want to link to someplace else.

Links usually require attributes.

href : "hypertext reference"

target:  Where you want the link to open. Use _blank to open in a new window.

 
<a href="https://www.anniecannons.org" target="_blank">Visit AnnieCannons</a>

wiki.title: Week 1 Overview
wiki.body_html:
This week, we will be learning the following:

  • Class policies, expectations, and our Community Agreements.
  • Review HTML and CodePen
  • Start to learn CSS, including colors, fonts, and the box model

 

Alt text is important!
wiki.module_title: Week 1: HTML review, Intro to CSS

wiki.title: Week 2 Overview
wiki.body_html:
This week, we'll continue working on our HTML and CSS skills. We'll also focus on:

  • Creating vertical navbars with semantic HTML and CSS
  • Turning our links into buttons
  • Build the Spacegaze landing page!

wiki.title: Week 3 Overview
wiki.body_html:
This week, we'll explore laying out web pages using CSS Grid. We'll also look at making sites responsive, meaning the same web page works on all kinds of devices (phones, tablets, desktop, and more):

  • Understanding CSS Grid (hint: parents and children!)
  • Annotating designs with Grid in mind
  • Understanding responsive design and media queries
  • Build the Spacegaze telescope detail page!

wiki.title: Week 4 Overview
wiki.body_html:
This week you'll be working on making horizontal navigation bars using Flexbox, another method for doing CSS layouts. 

You'll learn the difference between CSS Grid and Flexbox, and when to use each approach.

You'll also learn about CSS variables, which are very handy. Write your color once, refer to it everywhere else!

By the end of this week, you'll be ready to build entire web pages in CodePen!

wiki.title: Week 5 Overview
wiki.body_html:
This week is spent moving our code from CodePen to Visual Studio Code (or VS Code). VS Code is an industry-standard tool used for editing HTML, CSS, and JavaScript code (among other types of code).

Once our work is in VS Code, we have the ability to add our own images, create multiple page websites, and publishing those websites.

We will then connect VS Code to Github, an industry-standard tool for sharing and collaborating on code. To do this, we'll need to install some additional tools along the way.

Finally, we'll move our work to Github Pages, where your work will look like a "real" web page and not just code to read.

By the end of the week, you will be able to move your code from CodePen to VS Code to Github to Github Pages. There will be lots of practice!
wiki.module_title: Week 5: VS Code, Git, Github, Github Pages

wiki.title: Week 7 Overview
wiki.body_html:
This week, we start working in JavaScript.

The first two days are fairly light. We start to think about dynamic vs static content, identify JavaScript in action on websites, and explore what JavaScript looks like.

The second two days are focused on variables, one of the core concepts in JavaScript. You've already been introduced to the variable idea in CSS. JavaScript takes it a little further with different types of variables.

Meanwhile, you'll have some time to work on a bonus project, the Bento Grid, which includes a responsive grid-based layout and posting this on Github and Github Pages.

wiki.title: AnnieCannons Learner Journey
wiki.body_html:
🗺️ Build Your Personal Learning Map 

AnnieCannons Learner Journey   - From Intro to Programming through Frontend and Backend Development. Use this to help you track your growth, set goals, and reflect along the way.

✅ Your Action Step:

Download or copy this roadmap and create your own personalized version. This can be a Notion board, Google Doc, journal, or spreadsheet- whatever works best for your brain.

📌 What to Include:

  • 🗓️ Timeline: Add target dates for each milestone

  • ✅ Progress Tracker: Create a system to check off learning goals as you complete them

  • ✨ Outside Learning Goals: Include topics or skills you’re exploring beyond the AnnieCannons curriculum. Organize your goals by category,here are some suggestions:

    • Skills I Want to Build

    • Certifications or Courses

    • Side Projects

    • Outside Reading/Exploration

    Revisit + Revise: This is a living document- You will update it as you grow and change.

wiki.title: Creating Your Standout Resume
wiki.body_html:
Creating Your Standout Resume

These steps will help you create a compelling, industry-standard resume.

1️⃣ Explore Resume Templates

Task: Review the resume templates below and choose one that best fits your professional style.

 🔹Resume Template 1
 🔹 Resume Template 2
 🔹 Resume Template BEST PRACTICE
💡 Which template are you choosing? Why does it fit your style?

2️⃣ Draft Your Resume

Before formatting, outline your key sections:
 ✅ Education – Degrees, certifications, relevant coursework
 ✅ Skills – Technical & soft skills
 ✅ Experience – Jobs, internships, projects
 ✅ Achievements – Impact, awards, measurable results

📌 Action: Open Resume_Template, make a copy, and rename it:
 📁 yourfirstname-lastname-draftresume
💡 List at least 3 key experiences or skills you want to highlight in your resume

3️⃣ Choose & Format Your Resume

📌 Action: Select a template that fits your style, make a copy, and rename it:
 📁 yourfirstname-lastname-resume

💡 Alternative Option: If you're using Indeed’s Resume Builder, ensure your resume follows industry best practices. Once completed, download it as a PDF for submission and organization.

💡 What is one way you can format your resume to make it visually appealing and easy to read?

4️⃣ Stay Organized

📌 Action: Create a "Resumes" folder inside your Career Development drive folder. Store both your draft and all final versions in this folder.

5️⃣ Polish & Perfect

Your resume is a living document! Edit for clarity, impact, and relevance.

📌 Checklist for Refinement:
 ✅ Use strong action verbs ("developed," "optimized," "led")
 ✅ Quantify achievements where possible ("Increased efficiency by 30%")
 ✅ Keep it concise (1 page for early-career professionals)
 ✅ Ensure formatting is consistent and easy to read
💡 What’s one change you made to improve your resume?

6️⃣ Learn from the Pros

📌 Review these real-world examples for inspiration:
 📝 Harvard CV Early Career Template.docx
 📝 newgrad-resume-template-official-a.pdf (*DO NOT LOOK AT FORMAT-FOCUS ON CONTENT)
 📝 example resume
 📝entry-level-programmer
 📝Resume PROTIPS for Software Developers:
  Crafting Your Tech Resume: A Comprehensive Guide
💡 What’s one takeaway from reviewing these examples?

🎯 Final Step: Submit Your Resume! 🚀

Once your resume is polished and ready, submit your final version as yourfirstname-lastname-resume.pdf.

🎉 Great work! You’re one step closer to landing your dream job!

wiki.title: Job Platforms & Networking Resources
wiki.body_html:
Assignment: Exploring Online Job Platforms

Objective:

Your goal is to set up a profile and customize your job search filters on at least two different job boards. This will help you tailor your job search experience and optimize your chances of finding relevant opportunities.

Resources:

Be sure to check out AnnieCannons' [AnnieCannons JOB PLATFORMS ], which includes:

  • Online job platforms

  • Professional networking platforms

  • Additional resources for job seekers

Feel free to make a copy if you'd like to edit or customize it!

Instructions:

  1. Choose at least two job boards to create profiles on and set up customized job filters.

    • Popular options include: Indeed, LinkedIn, Glassdoor, Dice, AngelList, Hired, and Wellfound (formerly AngelList Talent).

  2. Adjust filters to match your job search preferences, such as:

    • Location (remote/in-office/hybrid)

    • Job type (full-time, part-time, contract, internship)

    • Industry or specific roles

  3. Take a screenshot of your customized job search settings for each platform.

  4. Submit your screenshots below.

Reminder:

Tech communities and forums are excellent ways to engage with like-minded professionals. They offer opportunities to:

  • Learn from others

  • Seek guidance on technical topics

  • Stay updated on industry trends and best practices

Consider joining platforms such as GitHub, Dev.to, Stack Overflow, or relevant Discord/Slack communities to expand your professional network!

wiki.title: Week 9 Overview
wiki.body_html:
This week, we'll continue to build on variables, looking at how variables are reassigned, template literals in console.log, pseudocoding hamburger menus, and dot and bracket notation.

Don't forget that Tuesday (Election Day) is a holiday so you can go vote!
